# Transcrição: 02 How to Create a Page Post & Find an Ad ID

- **Fonte:** 02 How to Create a Page Post & Find an Ad ID
- **Duração:** 03:45
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:03]

Okay, so I'm going to show you how to create a page post and also how to find an ad ID so that you can use the same creative, the same ad creative across different ad sets. Now remember the reason you might want to do that is to build social proof. So if you create different ads at each ad set you're driving budget into all sorts of different creative. If you use the same creative across different ad sets or campaigns all your budget is going towards a couple or even one piece of creative meaning you're going to rapidly build your likes comments and shares. So the first thing we want to do is click on this little hamburger on the left hand side and what we're going to do is go to page posts. So the first thing you're going to want to do is select the page that you want to run the dark post from. This is quite important so remember the right page is selected here. Now you'll see here we've got scheduled post, published post, and then ad post. So we're going to go create post under ad post and you'll see here this is essentially all the options that you normally have for a normal ad creative. So you can create just a photo with some text and an image. You can create a video with a piece of copy. You can create just a normal status so just some some copy and then probably the most popular one is the single image link click ad. So you're going to upload your image and add your link and your link headline. Now when you click on create post you'll notice here it says only use this post for an ad or you can also publish it to your page later and then you can schedule a time. Typically I just leave this as only use this for an ad unless I really want that on my news feed as well. And once I create this and you'll see here there's a column called ID. Now this is essentially the ad ID so you can copy this and what we can then do is when we go back into ads manager when we're at the ad level in inside of a campaign we can actually just click on enter post ID and you'll see here it's selected as use existing post so you can either create a new ad and if that doesn't show up just click on create new ad use existing post and then you can punch in the post here and you should also be able to select it from the drop down here. When you click submit it will show up. So that's one way to use the same post across your different ads. Another thing that you can do is let's say you already had an ad that you created and you created this at the ad level not as a page post and you want to use the same ad across a different ad set. Maybe you're testing something new out a different targeting and you really don't want to

## [03:03 - 03:45]

create a new ad because you're going to lose all those likes comments and shares. So we're going to go preview and we're going to preview this ad in the news feed by clicking on this little button here and going Facebook post with comments and you'll see once it comes up in the news feed there's a long string of numbers here. You'll copy this and that's essentially the ad ID as well. So again when you create the ad you can simply punch in that ad ID. So you'll go use existing post and punch this in here and you'll be able to click submit and it will pull that same ad through with all the likes comments and shares that were already on there.
